refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating mission context into measurable performance targets. This remains solution-neutral and avoids prescribing how derivation, recording, or management are realized.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that given a defined worksite mission context (inputs: site constraints, equipment type, operational goals, safety policies), the system produces a documented set of performance targets (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable back to mission inputs and satisfy acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site constraints, operational goals, equipment type, safety policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall derive quantitative performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall establish traceability between derived performance targets and worksite mission inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall record performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement versions and changes.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a mission-context capture module that accepts worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, environmental conditions) and applies a deterministic mapping function to derive performance targets; maintain a versioned baseline registry with change history and traceability links.

DP-B: Establish a parameterized performance-target template library indexed by worksite class/mission type; populate templates via structured intake of worksite safety and operational constraints; log all instantiations and calibration updates in an audit trail.

DP-C: Design a two-stage derivation pipeline: (1) parse and normalize worksite mission context into a canonical requirement model; (2) apply rule-based or algorithmic transformation to generate performance targets; persist all intermediate states and maintain a queryable history of baseline versions.

DP-D: Create a federated performance-target service that accepts worksite metadata and delegates derivation to domain-specific sub-modules (safety-driven targets, operational-policy-driven targets, environmental-constraint-driven targets); aggregate results and maintain unified traceability and versioning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Design a two-stage derivation pipeline: (1) parse and normalize worksite mission context into a canonical requirement model; (2) apply rule-based or algorithmic transformation to generate performance targets; persist all intermediate states and maintain a queryable history of baseline versions.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between normalization logic and transformation rules if both are monolithic; mitigated by separating parsing (stage 1) from derivation (stage 2).
Dependency on worksite input schema stability; if schema changes frequently, normalization module requires frequent updates. Recommend versioned input contracts.
History/versioning mechanism must not be tightly coupled to derivation logic; recommend separate persistence layer with query interface.
Risk of implicit coupling to specific safety-plan or operational-policy formats; mitigated by canonical model abstraction.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits lower coupling than DP-D (which introduces service-delegation complexity) and DP-A (which conflates capture and mapping). Two-stage design allows independent evolution of parsing and transformation. Canonical intermediate model decouples upstream (worksite inputs) from downstream (performance targets).

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured or semi-structured form (safety plan, operational policies are documented and accessible).
ASSUMPTION: A deterministic or rule-based transformation from mission context to performance targets is feasible and maintainable.
RISK: If worksite inputs are highly unstructured (free-text safety plans), normalization stage may require manual intervention or NLP, increasing complexity and reducing traceability.
RISK: If performance-target derivation rules are domain-specific and evolve frequently, rule maintenance becomes a bottleneck; recommend periodic review and versioning of rule sets.
RISK: History/baseline registry may grow unbounded; recommend retention policy and archival strategy.
ASSUMPTION: Traceability from worksite input → canonical model → performance target is required and auditable; this implies logging at both stage boundaries.
RISK: If worksite safety plan or operational policies conflict or are ambiguous, derivation logic must define conflict-resolution strategy; recommend explicit documentation of precedence rules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005 (refined): The NGHE solution shall implement a two-stage derivation pipeline that (1) normalizes worksite mission context (safety plan, operational policies, environmental constraints) into a canonical requirement model, and (2) applies documented transformation rules to generate performance targets. The system shall maintain a versioned baseline registry with full traceability from worksite inputs through intermediate canonical model to final performance targets, support audit queries, and log all calibration/baseline updates with timestamps and change rationale. Acceptance criteria: (a) All performance targets are traceable to at least one worksite input; (b) Baseline history is queryable by version, date, and change type; (c) Normalization and transformation stages are independently testable; (d) Conflict-resolution rules for ambiguous or contradictory inputs are documented and applied consistently.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized mission context parsing, normalization, requirement modeling, rule-based transformation pipeline, and versioned baseline persistence layer

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw worksite mission context, operational directives, and baseline requirement specifications from external tasking authority

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports derived performance targets, canonical requirement models, and queryable baseline version history to downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services)

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional: receives safety constraints during normalization; exports safety-critical performance targets derived from mission context

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: NGHE Solution is the logical home for mission-to-performance derivation, context normalization, and requirement modeling. The two-stage pipeline (parse/normalize → transform/generate) is cohesive within a single solution layer. Intermediate state persistence and versioned baseline history are core NGHE responsibilities. Minor diffusion: performance targets must be exported via Telemetry to Vehicle and Teleoperation; safety constraints flow bidirectionally via SafetyAlert.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a consumer of derived performance targets, not a producer. Placing derivation logic here violates separation of concerns and creates tight coupling between mission interpretation and vehicle control. Derivation would diffuse across multiple vehicle subsystems and duplicate logic if multiple vehicles exist.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is an operator interface and control channel, not a derivation engine. Mission context parsing and requirement transformation belong upstream of teleoperation. Placing this DP here conflates operator input with algorithmic derivation and creates unnecessary coupling to operator UI/UX concerns.

Weak fit: Safety Services consumes safety-critical performance targets but should not own the derivation pipeline. Safety constraints are inputs to normalization, not the primary driver. Placing derivation here would require Safety Services to understand mission context semantics beyond its domain, creating diffusion into non-safety concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-C (two-stage derivation pipeline with state persistence and versioned history) is best contained within NGHE Solution because: (1) mission context parsing and normalization are inherently solution-level concerns; (2) rule-based transformation from requirements to performance targets is a core NGHE responsibility; (3) intermediate state persistence and queryable baseline history are architectural features of the solution layer, not vehicle or teleoperation subsystems; (4) the pipeline naturally feeds downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) via well-defined interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry/SafetyAlert out).
containment_risks: Risk: If mission context normalization requires real-time vehicle state feedback, tight coupling to Vehicle telemetry could emerge. Mitigation: design normalization to accept only static mission parameters and historical baselines, not live vehicle state.

Risk: If rule-based transformation rules must be updated frequently based on operational feedback, versioning and persistence could become a bottleneck. Mitigation: design rule repository as a separate queryable artifact within NGHE Solution with independent update cadence.

Risk: If Safety Services requires veto power over derived performance targets, bidirectional SafetyAlert coupling could create circular dependencies. Mitigation: establish clear precedence: safety constraints are inputs to derivation, not post-hoc filters; Safety Services validates outputs but does not re-derive.

Risk: If multiple NGHE Solution instances exist (e.g., for different operational domains), baseline version history could fragment. Mitigation: centralize versioned baseline persistence in a single authoritative store accessible to all instances.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria explicitly require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baseline history. NGHE Solution as IM boundary directly owns both artifacts: (1) derivation pipeline produces traceable outputs; (2) versioned baseline persistence layer satisfies history requirement. Verification is straightforward: audit trail from Tasking input → canonical model → performance targets → Telemetry export can be instrumented and queried.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. FR-CR-NGHE-005 is unambiguously allocated to NGHE Solution IM. The two-stage pipeline (parse/normalize → transform/generate) is cohesive within a single module boundary. External interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry/SafetyAlert out) establish clear responsibility edges. No ambiguity about which module owns derivation logic or baseline persistence.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C directly and sufficiently addresses FR-CR-NGHE-005. The two-stage pipeline (1) parse/normalize worksite mission context into canonical model, (2) apply rule-based transformation to generate performance targets, with intermediate state persistence and queryable history, is a complete solution to the functional requirement. The DP is specific enough to guide implementation (rule-based vs. algorithmic choice is explicit), and the persistence/versioning aspect directly satisfies the acceptance criterion for baseline history maintenance.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: Performance targets exported via Telemetry interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Mitigation: establish Telemetry as a read-only broadcast channel; Vehicle and Teleoperation consume targets but do not feed back derivation logic. Confirm no circular dependency where Vehicle state influences mission context normalization.

MODERATE: SafetyAlert bidirectional coupling. Risk: Safety Services could attempt to re-derive or veto performance targets post-derivation, creating circular logic. Mitigation: document that safety constraints are inputs to normalization (stage 1), not post-hoc filters. Safety Services validates outputs but does not re-derive. Establish clear precedence in rule repository.

LOW-MODERATE: Rule repository update cadence. If transformation rules must be updated frequently based on operational feedback, versioning could become a bottleneck or create stale baseline states. Mitigation: design rule repository as a separately versioned artifact within NGHE Solution with independent update and rollback capability. Ensure rule changes do not retroactively invalidate historical baselines.

LOW: Multi-instance fragmentation. If multiple NGHE Solution instances exist across operational domains, baseline version history could fragment. Mitigation: centralize versioned baseline persistence in a single authoritative store (e.g., shared database or artifact repository) accessible to all NGHE instances. Establish global versioning scheme.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: EXCELLENT CONTAINMENT (0.92 score justified). NGHE Solution is the logical and architectural home for DP-C. Mission context parsing, normalization, and requirement modeling are inherently solution-level concerns, not vehicle or teleoperation subsystems. The two-stage pipeline is cohesive and self-contained. Intermediate state persistence and queryable baseline history are architectural features of the solution layer. Minor diffusion (0.08) is acceptable and well-managed via external interfaces: Tasking (in), Telemetry (out), SafetyAlert (bidirectional). No evidence of scattered responsibilities or duplicated logic across modules. Recommended: document interface contracts (Telemetry as read-only broadcast, SafetyAlert as constraint input + validation output) to prevent diffusion creep.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Explicitly document the rule repository as a separately versioned artifact within NGHE Solution. Define rule update cadence, rollback procedures, and impact on baseline history. Ensure rule changes do not invalidate historical baselines retroactively.

REVISION 2: Clarify SafetyAlert interface semantics: (a) safety constraints flow in during stage 1 (normalization); (b) derived safety-critical performance targets flow out; (c) Safety Services validates but does not re-derive. Document precedence: safety constraints are inputs, not post-hoc filters.

REVISION 3: Confirm Telemetry interface is read-only broadcast from NGHE Solution to Vehicle and Teleoperation. Explicitly prohibit feedback loops where Vehicle state influences mission context normalization. If real-time vehicle state is needed, design as a separate optional input channel with clear isolation from core derivation logic.

REVISION 4: If multiple NGHE Solution instances are planned, establish a centralized authoritative baseline version store (e.g., shared database, artifact repository, or configuration management system). Define global versioning scheme and synchronization protocol to prevent fragmentation.

REVISION 5: Add traceability requirement to DP-C: every performance target must include a trace link to the worksite mission context input and the rule(s) applied during transformation. This directly supports the acceptance criterion for traceability and enables audit/verification.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is excellent (0.92). However, the gate results surface five explicit, actionable revisions that must be incorporated before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. Given the high safety criticality of this requirement (performance targets directly influence vehicle behavior and safety-critical operations), these revisions are not optional refinements—they are essential clarifications that prevent downstream architectural debt and safety-critical diffusion risks. Specifically: (1) rule repository versioning and retroactive baseline invalidation must be resolved at this level; (2) SafetyAlert bidirectional coupling semantics must be locked down to prevent circular derivation logic; (3) Telemetry read-only broadcast contract must be explicit to prevent Vehicle state feedback loops; (4) multi-instance fragmentation must be addressed if applicable; (5) traceability requirements must be added to DP-C to satisfy acceptance criteria and enable verification. These are not decomposition-level concerns—they are design-level clarifications that strengthen the current FR-DP-IM mapping before sub-FRs are derived.

revise.why: DP-C (two-stage pipeline with persistence and versioning) is logically sound and sufficient, but its specification must be hardened with explicit interface contracts, rule repository governance, and traceability instrumentation. Revisions 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 from the gate results must be incorporated into the DP narrative to eliminate ambiguity and prevent diffusion creep during implementation. Specifically: (a) add rule repository as a separately versioned artifact with defined update cadence and rollback procedures; (b) clarify SafetyAlert semantics (constraints in, validation out, no re-derivation); (c) establish Telemetry as read-only broadcast with explicit prohibition on Vehicle state feedback; (d) define centralized baseline version store if multi-instance deployment is planned; (e) add traceability instrumentation requirement (every target must link to source mission context and applied rules). These revisions do not change the core DP logic but make it architecturally sound and safety-critical-ready.

assumptions: Rule repository updates are infrequent enough that versioning overhead is manageable; if updates are continuous, a separate revision cycle may be needed.
SafetyAlert interface is bidirectional but safety constraints are inputs to normalization, not post-hoc filters; this assumption must be validated with Safety Services stakeholders.
Telemetry interface is broadcast-only from NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation do not feed back derivation logic or mission context updates.

If multiple NGHE Solution instances are deployed, a centralized authoritative baseline store is feasible and acceptable (e.g., shared database, artifact repository, or configuration management system).
Traceability can be instrumented via audit trail logging and queryable metadata (e.g., trace links embedded in performance target records).
High safety criticality justifies explicit interface contracts and governance procedures; these are not over-specification but risk mitigation.
open_risks: MODERATE: Rule repository update cadence and retroactive baseline invalidation. If transformation rules are updated frequently, historical baselines could become stale or inconsistent. Mitigation: design rule repository with independent versioning and rollback; ensure rule changes do not retroactively invalidate historical baselines. Confirm with NGHE Solution architect.

MODERATE: SafetyAlert circular dependency. If Safety Services attempts to re-derive or veto performance targets post-derivation, circular logic could emerge. Mitigation: document that safety constraints are inputs to normalization (stage 1), not post-hoc filters. Establish clear precedence in rule repository. Confirm with Safety Services stakeholders.

MODERATE: Telemetry feedback loops. If Vehicle or Teleoperation feed back state information that influences mission context normalization, circular dependencies could emerge. Mitigation: establish Telemetry as read-only broadcast; if real-time vehicle state is needed, design as separate optional input channel with clear isolation. Confirm with Vehicle and Teleoperation architects.

LOW-MODERATE: Multi-instance fragmentation. If multiple NGHE Solution instances exist, baseline version history could fragment across instances. Mitigation: centralize versioned baseline persistence in single authoritative store. Confirm deployment topology with system architect.

LOW: Traceability instrumentation overhead. Audit trail logging and trace link metadata could introduce performance or storage overhead. Mitigation: design traceability as optional queryable feature (not real-time); batch audit logs; use efficient metadata encoding. Confirm with NGHE Solution performance requirements.
evidence_needed: Revised DP-C narrative incorporating all five revisions (rule repository governance, SafetyAlert semantics, Telemetry read-only contract, multi-instance baseline store, traceability instrumentation).

Interface control document (ICD) or contract specification for Tasking (input), Telemetry (output), and SafetyAlert (bidirectional) interfaces, with explicit semantics and prohibition on feedback loops.
Rule repository design specification: versioning scheme, update cadence, rollback procedures, impact on baseline history, and precedence rules for safety constraints.
Traceability requirement specification: every performance target must include trace link to source mission context input and applied rule(s); audit trail must be queryable and maintainable.
Confirmation from Safety Services, Vehicle, and Teleoperation stakeholders that interface contracts and circular dependency mitigations are acceptable.
Deployment topology confirmation: are multiple NGHE Solution instances planned? If yes, centralized baseline version store design and synchronization protocol.
Acceptance criteria verification plan: how will documented performance targets, baseline history, and traceability be audited and verified during integration and operational testing?
